steps.explanation: Identify the relationship type. The scatterplot shows points following a roughly straight path with no curvature, indicating a linear model. This eliminates choices C (\(y = -582(1.2)^x\)) and D (\(y = 582(1.2)^x\)), which are exponential and would produce curves.

Determine the sign of the slope. As \(x\) increases the plotted \(y\) values also increase, so the slope must be positive (+1.2). Choice B has slope −1.2 (a decreasing line), which contradicts the upward trend. Eliminated.
Only choice A remains: \(y = -582 + 1.2x\). Verify with a representative point near \((3500, 3700)\): \(y = -582 + 1.2(3500) = -582 + 4200 = 3618\), which closely matches the observed value.

• Choice A: \(y = -582 + 1.2x\) — linear with positive slope; fits the upward linear trend. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • Choice B: \(y = 582 - 1.2x\) — linear but with negative slope; predicts \(y\) decreasing as \(x\) increases. Contradicts the data. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: \(y = -582(1.2)^x\) — exponential and always negative for positive bases; produces a curve nowhere near a straight upward scatter. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: \(y = 582(1.2)^x\) — exponential growth; produces a steeply curving line, not the linear pattern shown. Eliminated.
